title: Good Neighbours and Jobs Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.
rounds.explanation: neighbour: A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Strong shoes that cover your feet and part of your legs.
rounds.explanation: boots: Strong shoes that cover your feet and part of your legs.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person who grows food and looks after farm animals.
rounds.explanation: farmer: A person who grows food and looks after farm animals.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who finds out news and writes or tells stories about it.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The special set of clothes that people wear for their job or school.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Doing something that makes a job easier or better for someone.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Theo is our neighbour, and he lives in the blue house.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The reporter wrote about market day for the town newspaper.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The firefighter wears a helmet to keep her head safe.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: In the story, how many passengers does Amal count on Nadia's bus?
rounds.explanation: Amal counts all the way from one to ten, then says that there are ten people on the bus.

rounds.prompt: In the story, what does Karim use to reach the high glass?
choices: A hose and a bucket of water
rounds.explanation: The story says Karim used a long pole. A hose belongs to the firefighters, and a tractor belongs to a farmer.

rounds.prompt: The bus driver wears a blue ______ to work every day.
rounds.explanation: A uniform is the set of clothes worn for a job. A helmet is worn by firefighters, and a tractor and a stethoscope are not clothes.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What two jobs does the text say firefighters do?
choices: They put out fires, and they rescue people who are trapped inside burning buildings.
The mask lets her breathe fresh air inside the smoke, and the gloves let her hold hot things without burning her hands.
rounds.answer: They put out fires, and they rescue people who are trapped inside burning buildings.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: They put out fires, and they rescue people who are trapped inside burning buildings.

rounds.prompt: What does the poem say the neighbours are doing?
choices: The mask lets her breathe fresh air inside the smoke, and the gloves let her hold hot things without burning her hands.
Helmets on their heads, heavy boots on their feet, thick gloves on their hands, and a mask on Leila's face.
The neighbours are helping other neighbours.
rounds.answer: The neighbours are helping other neighbours.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The neighbours are helping other neighbours.

rounds.prompt: Which four pieces of equipment do the two firefighters wear in the story?
choices: Karim brought water, Nadia swept the floor, Rami carried the heavy boxes, and the two firefighters helped clean the shop.
Helmets on their heads, heavy boots on their feet, thick gloves on their hands, and a mask on Leila's face.
Grandmas, grandpas, mums, dads and children.
rounds.answer: Helmets on their heads, heavy boots on their feet, thick gloves on their hands, and a mask on Leila's face.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Helmets on their heads, heavy boots on their feet, thick gloves on their hands, and a mask on Leila's face.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Describe a Job
rounds.target: Theo is our neighbour, and he lives in the blue house.

rounds.prompt: Speaking Game — Who Am I?
rounds.target: Play this guessing game with an adult or a friend.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — What Is Happening Now?
rounds.target: Describe six actions in the neighbourhood picture, one after the other.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The farmer grows maize and keeps six brown goats.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Adam is helping our neighbour carry a heavy box of books.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The firefighters are rescuing the baby goats behind the market.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'gloves' mean in this unit?
choices: Coverings for your hands, with a separate part for each finger.
A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.
A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.
rounds.answer: Coverings for your hands, with a separate part for each finger.
rounds.explanation: gloves: Coverings for your hands, with a separate part for each finger.

rounds.prompt: What does 'shopkeeper' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.
A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.
A person who owns or looks after a shop and sells things.
rounds.answer: A person who owns or looks after a shop and sells things.
rounds.explanation: shopkeeper: A person who owns or looks after a shop and sells things.

rounds.prompt: What does 'window cleaner' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who finds out news and writes or tells stories about it.
A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.
A person who lives near you, on your street or in your building.
rounds.answer: A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.
rounds.explanation: window cleaner: A person whose job is to clean the windows of houses and shops.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'nurse'?
rounds.explanation: nurse is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'growing'?
rounds.explanation: growing is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'firefighter'?
rounds.explanation: firefighter is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A person who keeps people safe and helps everyone follow the rules.
A person whose job is to drive a bus and carry passengers safely.
A hard hat that protects your head from knocks and falling things.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: A covering that goes over your nose and mouth to protect them.
A person who helps people who are ill and makes them better.
A person whose job is to help other people learn new things.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Doing something that makes a job easier or better for someone.
Saving a person or an animal from danger and bringing them somewhere safe.
Making a car, bus or lorry move and steering it along the road.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which of these questions asks about an action?
choices: What is the police officer doing?
Who is the firefighter helping today?
rounds.answer: What is the police officer doing?
rounds.explanation: An action is a thing and not a person, so it takes What. The other three questions all ask about a person.

rounds.prompt: The firefighters ______ climbing the ladder.
rounds.explanation: Firefighters means more than one person, so the helping word is are. Am belongs with I, and is belongs with one person.

rounds.prompt: Which is the correct -ing form of the verb drive?
rounds.explanation: Drive ends in a silent e, so the e is dropped before -ing. Drived is not a word, and the v is never doubled.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word neighbour mean?
choices: A person who puts out fires and rescues people.
A person who owns a shop and sells things.
A person who lives near you.
A hard hat that protects the head.
rounds.answer: A person who lives near you.
rounds.explanation: A neighbour lives close to your home. The other options describe a firefighter, a shopkeeper and a helmet.

rounds.prompt: The ______ uses a long pole to reach the high windows.
rounds.explanation: Only a person can use a pole. A helmet and a uniform are clothing, and a market square is a place.

rounds.prompt: Which piece of equipment helps a firefighter breathe fresh air inside smoke?
rounds.explanation: The mask covers the nose and mouth. A badge and a whistle belong to a police officer, and a tractor belongs to a farmer.
